title: rubus incisus subvar. formosanus
intro: Rubus incisus subvar. formosanus, also known as the Formosan thimbleberry or Taiwanese thimbleberry, is a variant of the Rubus incisus species, a type of bramble or thimbleberry plant native to Taiwan. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. The plant can be used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide relief from various health conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Rubus incisus subvar. formosanus for medicinal use.
